sections.paragraph:
ε´ numerical sign = 5 (πέντε) or 5th (πέμπτη); Ac 19:9 D. In titles: Hv 5; m 5; Hs 5 (Apollon. Paradox. 46 Θεόφραστος ἐν τῇ ε´ τῶν φυτικῶν αἰτιῶν φησίν; freq. pap, e.g. PWisc II, 80, 15 al.).

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Hv Hv = Visions

m m = Mandates

Hs Hs = Similitudes

freq. freq. = frequent(ly)

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

e.g. e.g. = exempli gratia (for example)

PWisc PWisc = The Wisconsin Papyri—List 4

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 267
